# Storyteller

> Lightweight TypeScript logging library that treats logs as stories: grouped notes emitted as a single structured event.

## What it does

Storyteller collects timestamped notes during an operation and emits them as a single structured story event at one of three levels: tell (info), warn, or oops (error). Events are delivered to pluggable audiences — a console audience prints colorized reports, a DB audience stores JSON records. Zero production dependencies.

## Quick start

```bash
npm install @lovelaces-io/storyteller
```

```typescript
import { Storyteller } from "@lovelaces-io/storyteller";

const story = new Storyteller({ origin: { who: "my-service" } });
story.note("Connected to database");
story.note("Loaded 42 records");
story.tell("Startup complete");
```

## Key concepts

- **Stories** — a group of notes emitted as one event with a title and level
- **Notes** — timestamped log entries with optional context (who, what, where, error)
- **Levels** — `tell` (info), `warn` (warning), `oops` (error)
- **Audiences** — pluggable listeners that receive story events (console, DB, or custom)
- **Reports** — human-readable formatted text output from story events
- **Origin** — optional who/what/where metadata attached to every story from an instance

## API surface

- `Storyteller` — core class; collects notes and emits story events
  - `.note(text, context?)` — add a timestamped note with optional who/what/where/error
  - `.tell(title)` — emit story at info level
  - `.warn(title)` — emit story at warning level
  - `.oops(title, error?)` — emit story at error level with optional error object
  - `.reset()` — clear accumulated notes without emitting
  - `.summarize(options?)` — preview current notes as formatted summary without emitting
  - `.audience` — registry to add/remove/query audience members
    - `.audience.has(name)` — check if an audience member is registered by name
    - `.audience.names()` — list all registered audience member names
  - `.to(...names)` — target specific audiences (call synchronously after tell/warn/oops)
- `useStoryteller(options?)` — get or create a shared singleton instance
- `formatStory(event, options?)` — format a single story event as a human-readable report
- `writeStoryReport(stories[], options?)` — format multiple story events grouped by day
- `consoleAudience()` — audience that prints color-coded stories to the console (registered by default)
- `dbAudience(insert)` — audience that persists warn/oops events via a provided insert function
- `ANSI` — ANSI color code constants
- `getLevelColor(level)` — get the ANSI color for a story level
- `formatOrigin(origin?)` — format an origin object as a readable string
- `formatDuration(ms)` — format a duration in milliseconds as a human-readable string
- `getLevelLabel(level)` — get the human-readable label for a story level (e.g., "Information", "Warning", "Error")

### Types

- `StoryLevel` — `"tell" | "warn" | "oops"`
- `StoryNote` — timestamped note with optional context
- `StoryEventBase` — core event structure (timestamp, level, levelLabel, title, origin, notes, error)
- `StoryEvent` — event with attached `.summarize()` method
- `AudienceMember` — audience interface (name, accepts?, hear)
- `FormattedReport` — formatted report result (text + structured data)
- `StoryReport` — structured report data
- `ReportNote` — a note as it appears in a formatted report
- `ReportOptions` — options for report formatting (timezone, locale, detail, noteLimit, colors)
- `PreviewOptions` — options for the `.summarize()` preview method
- `StoryReportOptions` — options for writeStoryReport
- Deprecated aliases (still exported for backwards compatibility): `StorySummaryOptions` (use `ReportOptions`), `StorySummary` (use `FormattedReport`), `SummaryNote` (use `ReportNote`)

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@lovelaces-io/storyteller
- GitHub: https://github.com/lovelaces-io/storyteller
- Issues: https://github.com/lovelaces-io/storyteller/issues
